title: Water-quality and algal-bloom monitoring for ports and aquaculture
summary: Ocean-colour and biogeochemistry data provide early warning of algal blooms and water-quality issues.
description:
Harmful algal blooms threaten aquaculture, bathing waters and port operations. Ground sampling is sparse and slow, so blooms are often detected only after damage occurs.

CMEMS biogeochemistry products and Sentinel-3 OLCI ocean-colour data track chlorophyll and turbidity, providing early warning of bloom development. Operators can take protective action for fish farms, beaches and water intakes before impacts escalate.

site.integration_detail: CMEMS biogeochemistry and Sentinel-3 ocean-colour data are processed into chlorophyll and turbidity indicators with thresholds for bloom alerts.
